title: Verbascum masguindalii
intro: Verbascum masguindalii, also known as Mulein or Mullein, is a biennial plant belonging to the Scrophulariaceae family, characterized by its large, velvety leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its expectorant properties, which aid in relieving respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Verbascum masguindalii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the most common preparations made from this plant are infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and wounds.
